name: Tommy Benford
abstract: Thomas Benford (April 19, 1905 – March 24, 1994) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: Bob Greene's "World of Jelly Roll Morton"
Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Django Reinhardt

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: My ole pal Dickie Wells on trombone, Tommy Benford, drums, and Jimmie(Slim)Rodgers, an-ex-Benny Carter pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: I would be remiss if I didn't bring up the historic recording session of April 1937 in Paris with the four saxophones... There was Tommy Benford [phonetic] on drums. [INTERVIEWEE] On the drums, yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And then you'd take Tommy Benford. Who would you take on bass? [INTERVIEWEE] Tommy -- I would take (unintelligible) bass, couldn't get George Dupvier...

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Tommy Benford, but the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not respond or provide any personal connection.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: Tommy Benford was the drummer in the band... Tommy Benford took over... Then Jelly Roll Morton came.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: Tommy Benford. And we went to -- went to -- from there down -- we went to about 15 cities in Italy
